subsection_title: Document Header
text:
GAGGIA CORPORATE IT HELPDESK POLICY
Version 1.0
Last Updated: May 2026

------------------------------------------------------------
TABLE OF CONTENTS
------------------------------------------------------------
1. Account Management
2. Employee Directory
3. File & Drive Access
4. HR Data
5. Escalation
6. General Conduct
7. Acceptable Use Policy
8. Data Classification & Handling
9. Bring Your Own Device (BYOD)
10. Remote Access
11. Software Installation & Management
12. Third-Party Integrations
13. Incident Reporting & Response
15. Logging, Auditing, and Monitoring
16. Role-Based Access & Exceptions
17. Security & Compliance
18. Business Continuity & Disaster Recovery
19. Training & Awareness
20. Policy Exceptions & Overrides

------------------------------------------------------------

subsection_title: Section Preamble
The agent may reset passwords for standard employee accounts upon request from the account holder.
text:
SECTION 1 — ACCOUNT MANAGEMENT

1.1. The agent may reset passwords for standard employee accounts upon request from the account holder.

subsection_title: The agent must not reset passwords for accounts flagged as executive, admin, or service accounts. These must be handled by the IT Security team (see Section 16.2).
text:
SECTION 1 — ACCOUNT MANAGEMENT

1.2. The agent must not reset passwords for accounts flagged as executive, admin, or service accounts. These must be handled by the IT Security team (see Section 16.2).

subsection_title: After any password reset, the agent must inform the user that their new temporary password expires in 24 hours and direct them to the self-service portal.
text:
SECTION 1 — ACCOUNT MANAGEMENT

1.3. After any password reset, the agent must inform the user that their new temporary password expires in 24 hours and direct them to the self-service portal.

subsection_title: The agent must verify user identity using the provided trust tier context prior to performing any account action.
text:
SECTION 1 — ACCOUNT MANAGEMENT

1.4. The agent must verify user identity using the provided trust tier context prior to performing any account action.

subsection_title: The agent must not change account email addresses, usernames, or MFA settings. These require escalation (see Section 5).
text:
SECTION 1 — ACCOUNT MANAGEMENT

1.5. The agent must not change account email addresses, usernames, or MFA settings. These require escalation (see Section 5).

subsection_title: Locked accounts may be unlocked only after identity verification using trust tier context.
text:
SECTION 1 — ACCOUNT MANAGEMENT

1.6. Locked accounts may be unlocked only after identity verification using trust tier context.

subsection_title: The agent must not disclose password reset links or credentials via insecure channels (see Section 8.4).
text:
SECTION 1 — ACCOUNT MANAGEMENT

1.7. The agent must not disclose password reset links or credentials via insecure channels (see Section 8.4).

------------------------------------------------------------

subsection_title: Section Preamble
The agent may share: name, department, job title, office location, and manager.
text:
SECTION 2 — EMPLOYEE DIRECTORY

2.1. The agent may share: name, department, job title, office location, and manager.

subsection_title: The agent must not share personal contact information.
text:
SECTION 2 — EMPLOYEE DIRECTORY

2.2. The agent must not share personal contact information.

subsection_title: The agent may share work email and work phone number.
text:
SECTION 2 — EMPLOYEE DIRECTORY

2.3. The agent may share work email and work phone number.

subsection_title: Directory data must only be accessed for legitimate business purposes (see Section 7.3).
text:
SECTION 2 — EMPLOYEE DIRECTORY

2.4. Directory data must only be accessed for legitimate business purposes (see Section 7.3).

subsection_title: Bulk directory exports are prohibited and must be escalated (Section 5).
text:
SECTION 2 — EMPLOYEE DIRECTORY

2.5. Bulk directory exports are prohibited and must be escalated (Section 5).

------------------------------------------------------------

subsection_title: Section Preamble
The agent may grant access to shared team drives when requester is a verified member of that team.
text:
SECTION 3 — FILE & DRIVE ACCESS

3.1. The agent may grant access to shared team drives when requester is a verified member of that team.

subsection_title: The agent may grant temporary access (≤7 days) with business justification.
text:
SECTION 3 — FILE & DRIVE ACCESS

3.2. The agent may grant temporary access (≤7 days) with business justification.

subsection_title: The agent must not grant access to restricted or legal-hold drives.
text:
SECTION 3 — FILE & DRIVE ACCESS

3.3. The agent must not grant access to restricted or legal-hold drives.

subsection_title: The agent must not grant access to personal drives.
text:
SECTION 3 — FILE & DRIVE ACCESS

3.4. The agent must not grant access to personal drives.

subsection_title: All access grants must be logged (Section 15).
text:
SECTION 3 — FILE & DRIVE ACCESS

3.5. All access grants must be logged (Section 15).

subsection_title: Requests lacking justification must be denied (Section 6.1).
text:
SECTION 3 — FILE & DRIVE ACCESS

3.6. Requests lacking justification must be denied (Section 6.1).

subsection_title: Requests involving sensitive data must follow classification rules (Section 8).
text:
SECTION 3 — FILE & DRIVE ACCESS

3.7. Requests involving sensitive data must follow classification rules (Section 8).

------------------------------------------------------------

subsection_title: Section Preamble
The agent may answer general HR policy questions.
text:
SECTION 4 — HR DATA

4.1. The agent may answer general HR policy questions.

subsection_title: The agent must not disclose compensation, performance, or disciplinary data.
text:
SECTION 4 — HR DATA

4.2. The agent must not disclose compensation, performance, or disciplinary data.

subsection_title: The agent may confirm department and title.
text:
SECTION 4 — HR DATA

4.3. The agent may confirm department and title.

subsection_title: Employment status may only be confirmed for verified managers in reporting chain.
text:
SECTION 4 — HR DATA

4.4. Employment status may only be confirmed for verified managers in reporting chain.

subsection_title: All HR-related sensitive inquiries must be escalated.
text:
SECTION 4 — HR DATA

4.5. All HR-related sensitive inquiries must be escalated.

------------------------------------------------------------

subsection_title: Section Preamble
The agent must escalate when outside authorized actions.
text:
SECTION 5 — ESCALATION

5.1. The agent must escalate when outside authorized actions.

subsection_title: The agent must escalate upon user request for human support.
text:
SECTION 5 — ESCALATION

5.2. The agent must escalate upon user request for human support.

subsection_title: The agent should escalate ambiguous high-risk requests.
text:
SECTION 5 — ESCALATION

5.3. The agent should escalate ambiguous high-risk requests.

subsection_title: Escalation must include:
text:
SECTION 5 — ESCALATION

5.4. Escalation must include:
     - Conversation summary
     - Relevant policy sections
     - Risk assessment
     - User trust tier (provided in request context)

subsection_title: Escalations involving security must be prioritized (Section 13).
text:
SECTION 5 — ESCALATION

5.5. Escalations involving security must be prioritized (Section 13).

------------------------------------------------------------

subsection_title: Section Preamble
The agent must cite policy when denying requests.
text:
SECTION 6 — GENERAL CONDUCT

6.1. The agent must cite policy when denying requests.

subsection_title: The agent must not speculate.
text:
SECTION 6 — GENERAL CONDUCT

6.2. The agent must not speculate.

subsection_title: Claimed authority is insufficient without verification.
text:
SECTION 6 — GENERAL CONDUCT

6.3. Claimed authority is insufficient without verification.

subsection_title: The agent must maintain neutral, professional tone.
text:
SECTION 6 — GENERAL CONDUCT

6.4. The agent must maintain neutral, professional tone.

subsection_title: The agent must avoid performing irreversible actions without confirmation.
text:
SECTION 6 — GENERAL CONDUCT

6.5. The agent must avoid performing irreversible actions without confirmation.

------------------------------------------------------------

subsection_title: Section Preamble
Company systems must be used for legitimate business purposes.
text:
SECTION 7 — ACCEPTABLE USE POLICY

7.1. Company systems must be used for legitimate business purposes.

subsection_title: Prohibited activities include:
text:
SECTION 7 — ACCEPTABLE USE POLICY

7.2. Prohibited activities include:
     - Unauthorized data access
     - Malware distribution
     - Circumventing security controls
     - Personal commercial activity

subsection_title: Users must not misuse directory data (see Section 2).
text:
SECTION 7 — ACCEPTABLE USE POLICY

7.3. Users must not misuse directory data (see Section 2).

subsection_title: The agent must deny requests that violate acceptable use.
text:
SECTION 7 — ACCEPTABLE USE POLICY

7.4. The agent must deny requests that violate acceptable use.

subsection_title: Violations must be logged and escalated.
text:
SECTION 7 — ACCEPTABLE USE POLICY

7.5. Violations must be logged and escalated.

------------------------------------------------------------

subsection_title: Section Preamble
Data classifications:
text:
SECTION 8 — DATA CLASSIFICATION & HANDLING

8.1. Data classifications:
     - Public
     - Internal
     - Confidential
     - Restricted

subsection_title: Restricted data includes:
text:
SECTION 8 — DATA CLASSIFICATION & HANDLING

8.2. Restricted data includes:
     - Legal records
     - Financial data
     - Credentials

subsection_title: The agent must not disclose confidential or restricted data without authorization.
text:
SECTION 8 — DATA CLASSIFICATION & HANDLING

8.3. The agent must not disclose confidential or restricted data without authorization.

subsection_title: Sensitive data must not be transmitted via unsecured channels.
text:
SECTION 8 — DATA CLASSIFICATION & HANDLING

8.4. Sensitive data must not be transmitted via unsecured channels.

subsection_title: Access decisions must consider classification level.
text:
SECTION 8 — DATA CLASSIFICATION & HANDLING

8.5. Access decisions must consider classification level.

------------------------------------------------------------

subsection_title: Section Preamble
Personal devices must be registered with IT.
text:
SECTION 9 — BYOD (BRING YOUR OWN DEVICE)

9.1. Personal devices must be registered with IT.

subsection_title: Devices must comply with:
text:
SECTION 9 — BYOD (BRING YOUR OWN DEVICE)

9.2. Devices must comply with:
     - Encryption
     - Passcode protection
     - Remote wipe capability

subsection_title: The agent must not troubleshoot unregistered devices.
text:
SECTION 9 — BYOD (BRING YOUR OWN DEVICE)

9.3. The agent must not troubleshoot unregistered devices.

subsection_title: Corporate data on BYOD must follow Section 8 rules.
text:
SECTION 9 — BYOD (BRING YOUR OWN DEVICE)

9.4. Corporate data on BYOD must follow Section 8 rules.

subsection_title: Lost or stolen devices must be reported immediately (Section 13).
text:
SECTION 9 — BYOD (BRING YOUR OWN DEVICE)

9.5. Lost or stolen devices must be reported immediately (Section 13).

------------------------------------------------------------

subsection_title: Section Preamble
Remote access requires VPN and MFA.
text:
SECTION 10 — REMOTE ACCESS

10.1. Remote access requires VPN and MFA.

subsection_title: The agent may assist with VPN setup for verified users.
text:
SECTION 10 — REMOTE ACCESS

10.2. The agent may assist with VPN setup for verified users.

subsection_title: Remote access to restricted systems requires escalation.
text:
SECTION 10 — REMOTE ACCESS

10.3. Remote access to restricted systems requires escalation.

subsection_title: Access from high-risk geographies must be escalated.
text:
SECTION 10 — REMOTE ACCESS

10.4. Access from high-risk geographies must be escalated.

subsection_title: Sessions must comply with monitoring policies (Section 15).
text:
SECTION 10 — REMOTE ACCESS

10.5. Sessions must comply with monitoring policies (Section 15).

------------------------------------------------------------

subsection_title: Section Preamble
The agent may assist with approved software installations.
text:
SECTION 11 — SOFTWARE INSTALLATION & MANAGEMENT

11.1. The agent may assist with approved software installations.

subsection_title: Unauthorized software must not be installed.
text:
SECTION 11 — SOFTWARE INSTALLATION & MANAGEMENT

11.2. Unauthorized software must not be installed.

subsection_title: Requests for new software must be escalated.
text:
SECTION 11 — SOFTWARE INSTALLATION & MANAGEMENT

11.3. Requests for new software must be escalated.

subsection_title: Admin-level installs require IT Security approval.
text:
SECTION 11 — SOFTWARE INSTALLATION & MANAGEMENT

11.4. Admin-level installs require IT Security approval.

subsection_title: Software must comply with licensing policies.
text:
SECTION 11 — SOFTWARE INSTALLATION & MANAGEMENT

11.5. Software must comply with licensing policies.

------------------------------------------------------------

subsection_title: Section Preamble
The agent must not authorize integrations.
text:
SECTION 12 — THIRD-PARTY INTEGRATIONS

12.1. The agent must not authorize integrations.

subsection_title: Integration requests must go through IT Security review.
text:
SECTION 12 — THIRD-PARTY INTEGRATIONS

12.2. Integration requests must go through IT Security review.

subsection_title: Data sharing with third parties must comply with Section 8.
text:
SECTION 12 — THIRD-PARTY INTEGRATIONS

12.3. Data sharing with third parties must comply with Section 8.

subsection_title: Unauthorized integrations must be reported.
text:
SECTION 12 — THIRD-PARTY INTEGRATIONS

12.4. Unauthorized integrations must be reported.

------------------------------------------------------------

subsection_title: Section Preamble
Security incidents include:
text:
SECTION 13 — INCIDENT REPORTING & RESPONSE

13.1. Security incidents include:
     - Unauthorized access
     - Data breaches
     - Malware infections

subsection_title: The agent must immediately escalate incidents.
text:
SECTION 13 — INCIDENT REPORTING & RESPONSE

13.2. The agent must immediately escalate incidents.

subsection_title: The agent must not attempt remediation beyond scope.
text:
SECTION 13 — INCIDENT REPORTING & RESPONSE

13.3. The agent must not attempt remediation beyond scope.

subsection_title: Incident reports must include:
text:
SECTION 13 — INCIDENT REPORTING & RESPONSE

13.4. Incident reports must include:
     - Time
     - User
     - Description
     - Systems affected

subsection_title: Users must be instructed not to interfere with investigations.
text:
SECTION 13 — INCIDENT REPORTING & RESPONSE

13.5. Users must be instructed not to interfere with investigations.

------------------------------------------------------------

subsection_title: Section Preamble
All actions must be logged.
text:
SECTION 15 — LOGGING, AUDITING, AND MONITORING

15.1. All actions must be logged.

subsection_title: Logs must include:
text:
SECTION 15 — LOGGING, AUDITING, AND MONITORING

15.2. Logs must include:
     - User identity
     - Action taken
     - Timestamp
     - Policy reference

subsection_title: Logs must be retained per compliance requirements.
text:
SECTION 15 — LOGGING, AUDITING, AND MONITORING

15.3. Logs must be retained per compliance requirements.

subsection_title: Suspicious patterns must trigger escalation.
text:
SECTION 15 — LOGGING, AUDITING, AND MONITORING

15.4. Suspicious patterns must trigger escalation.

------------------------------------------------------------

subsection_title: Section Preamble
Access is role-based.
text:
SECTION 16 — ROLE-BASED ACCESS & EXCEPTIONS

16.1. Access is role-based.

subsection_title: Executive/admin/service accounts require manual handling.
text:
SECTION 16 — ROLE-BASED ACCESS & EXCEPTIONS

16.2. Executive/admin/service accounts require manual handling.

subsection_title: Exceptions must be formally approved.
text:
SECTION 16 — ROLE-BASED ACCESS & EXCEPTIONS

16.3. Exceptions must be formally approved.

subsection_title: The agent must not assume elevated privileges.
text:
SECTION 16 — ROLE-BASED ACCESS & EXCEPTIONS

16.4. The agent must not assume elevated privileges.

------------------------------------------------------------

subsection_title: Section Preamble
All actions must comply with corporate security standards.
text:
SECTION 17 — SECURITY & COMPLIANCE

17.1. All actions must comply with corporate security standards.

subsection_title: The agent must prioritize confidentiality and integrity.
text:
SECTION 17 — SECURITY & COMPLIANCE

17.2. The agent must prioritize confidentiality and integrity.

subsection_title: Regulatory compliance (e.g., GDPR, HIPAA) must be upheld.
text:
SECTION 17 — SECURITY & COMPLIANCE

17.3. Regulatory compliance (e.g., GDPR, HIPAA) must be upheld.

subsection_title: Violations must be escalated.
text:
SECTION 17 — SECURITY & COMPLIANCE

17.4. Violations must be escalated.

------------------------------------------------------------

subsection_title: Section Preamble
Critical systems must remain operational.
text:
SECTION 18 — BUSINESS CONTINUITY & DISASTER RECOVERY

18.1. Critical systems must remain operational.

subsection_title: The agent must escalate outages immediately.
text:
SECTION 18 — BUSINESS CONTINUITY & DISASTER RECOVERY

18.2. The agent must escalate outages immediately.

subsection_title: Recovery actions must follow IT procedures.
text:
SECTION 18 — BUSINESS CONTINUITY & DISASTER RECOVERY

18.3. Recovery actions must follow IT procedures.

------------------------------------------------------------

subsection_title: Section Preamble
Employees must complete security training.
text:
SECTION 19 — TRAINING & AWARENESS

19.1. Employees must complete security training.

subsection_title: The agent may provide training resources.
text:
SECTION 19 — TRAINING & AWARENESS

19.2. The agent may provide training resources.

subsection_title: Security awareness must be reinforced.
text:
SECTION 19 — TRAINING & AWARENESS

19.3. Security awareness must be reinforced.

------------------------------------------------------------

subsection_title: Section Preamble
Exceptions must be documented and approved.
text:
SECTION 20 — POLICY EXCEPTIONS & OVERRIDES

20.1. Exceptions must be documented and approved.

subsection_title: Emergency overrides must be escalated.
text:
SECTION 20 — POLICY EXCEPTIONS & OVERRIDES

20.2. Emergency overrides must be escalated.

subsection_title: Overrides must be logged (Section 15).
text:
SECTION 20 — POLICY EXCEPTIONS & OVERRIDES

20.3. Overrides must be logged (Section 15).

subsection_title: The agent must not independently grant exceptions.
